foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Esophageal wall layers and sites of narrowing determine depth and distribution of caustic burns

foundations.subject: surgery
foundations.reason: Progressive dysphagia from stricture formation is the hallmark late complication of caustic injury

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Full-thickness caustic burns can cause acute perforation requiring emergency management

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Severe caustic strictures unresponsive to dilatation require esophageal replacement (colon/gastric interposition)

clinical.subject: surgery
clinical.reason: Caustic strictures carry long-term risk of squamous cell carcinoma (1000x increased risk)

objectives: SU28.6: Describe the clinical features, investigations and principles of
SU28.5: Describe the applied anatomy and physiology of esophagus
integration: This postgraduate-level roadmap connects Caustic Injury of Esophagus across foundational sciences and clinical applications.
